LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. CROSS COMPLAINANT LINDA CATRON'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY AND TO IMPOSE SANCTIONS - THE COURT ADOPTS THE RECOMMENDATION OF PRO TEM THORNLEY AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED. PLAINTIFF ORDERED TO RESPOND TO DISCOVERY REQUESTS REFERENCED IN THE MOTION AND PAY MOVING PARTY SANCTIONS OF $2,420 WITHIN FIFTEEN (15) DAYS. PLAINTIFF REPRESENTED AT THE HEARING THAT HE SERVED RESPONSES. DEFENDANT DENIES RECEIVING RESPONSES. PLAINTIFF OFFERED NO PROOF OF SERVICE. PLAINTIFF COULD NOT STATE WITH CERTAINTY ON WHAT DATE SERVICE OF RESPONSES OCCURRED. PLAINTIFF REPRESENTED SERVICE WAS EFFECTED WITHIN THE LAST "NINE (9) DAYS." EVEN IF TRUE, SERVICE WOULD HAVE OCCURRED AFTER DEFENDANT FILED THE INSTANT MOTION. THUS, SANCTIONS OF $2,420 ARE WARRANTED (5 HOURS @ $450/HR., PLUS $170 COSTS). BECAUSE PLAINTIFF PRODUCED NO EVIDENCE OF SERVICE, AN ORDER COMPELLING RESPONSES IS WARRANTED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR # 5976

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Pltf'S Responses To Discovery And To Impose Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-08-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. PAUL A. FRASSETTO, TEL. NO. 354-2712 APPEARING FOR THE CROSS-COMPLAINANT LINDA CATRON;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS COMPLAINANT LINDA CATRON'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Responses To Discovery And To Impose Sanctions - The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Thornley as follows: GRANTED. Plaintiff ordered to respond to discovery requests referenced in the motion and pay moving party sanctions of $2,420 within fifteen (15) days. Plaintiff represented at the hearing that he served responses. Defendant denies receiving responses. Plaintiff offered no proof of service. Plaintiff could not state with certainty on what date service of responses occurred. Plaintiff represented service was effected within the last "nine (9) days." Even if true, service would have occurred after defendant filed the instant motion. Thus, sanctions of $2,420 are warranted (5 hours @ $450/hr., plus $170 costs). Because plaintiff produced no evidence of service, an order compelling responses is warranted. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)
